category_description: ESTABLISHMENT - Formal arrangements for the establishment of a new school, including community engagement, planning, constitution and events surrounding the opening of the school.
examples_of_records: Certificate of Incorporation; Memorandum & Articles of Association; planning documentation; records of community consultation; documentation of the opening; photographs

category_description: CLOSURE - Formal arrangements for the closure of a school. For administrative arrangements (e.g. staff, disposal of assets), use the relevant topic under ADMINISTRATION.
examples_of_records: Legal documents

category_description: COUNCIL and COMMITTEES - Structure, membership, deliberations and operation of the school's governing Council (or equivalent) and committees, senior management group, and Student Council. For regular or subject-specific meetings relating to different aspects of the operation of the school and its services, use the relevant subject (e.g. TEACHING AND LEARNING).
examples_of_records: Includes constitution; terms of reference; election documents; appointments; papers and minutes of Council/Board and senior committee meetings

category_description: REGULATION - Accreditation, registration,  licensing, approval and regulation of the school to be an educational provider, including family day care educator.   Includes registration as a student exchange organisation, a Registered Training Organisation and as an International Baccalaureate school. Includes its approvals for courses. For the registration of teachers use ADMINISTRATION - HUMAN RESOURCES - APPOINTMENTS.
examples_of_records: Applications for registration and supporting documentation; approvals; renewals; information requests; cancellations; suspensions

category_description: COMPLIANCE MONITORING - Formal review against standards of the governance, enrolments, curriculum and student performance, student welfare, staff employment and school infrastructure.  Includes external and internal assessment or review.  For routine financial or performance audits, use ADMINISTRATION - FINANCE - AUDIT.
examples_of_records: Guidelines and standards; formal inspections, reviews, audits; results of assessments against standards

category_description: PLANNING - Strategic planning, reporting and reviewing or evaluating processes, procedures and school standards.
examples_of_records: Strategic plans; business plans; documentation of internal reviews

category_description: DELEGATIONS & AUTHORISATIONS - Delegations of power to staff to authorise financial or other decision-making and transactions.
REGISTER - Summary of all agreements/contracts entered into by the school
examples_of_records: Contracts register or system

category_description: SUCCESSFUL - MAJOR - Negotiation, establishment and maintenance of major agreements with external organisations relating to the provision of services. Examples include: educational or student welfare;  out of hours care; accommodation services; major buildings and works; funding; uniform shop; book shop; printing, cloud-based information systems.   For staff employment agreements (or similar) use ADMINISTRATION - HUMAN RESOURCES - AGREEMENTS.
disposal_action: Destroy 15 years after agreement/ contract expires
examples_of_records: Specifications; tender and negotiation records; quotations; assessments of tenders; final agreements/ contracts; service level agreements

category_description: SUCCESSFUL MINOR - Establishment and maintenance of minor agreements with external organisations for small scale-service provision.
examples_of_records: Final agreements/ contracts; service level agreements; tender and negotiation records

category_description: UNSUCCESSFUL - unsuccessful tenders or a tender/contracting process where there is no suitable provider, or where the tender/contracting process has been discontinued
disposal_action: Destroy 2 years after tender process completed or discontinued

category_description: SUBMISSIONS - Submissions to external organisations, including regulatory bodies and government inquiries, on issues relevant to the core responsibilities of the school. For advice provided to parents or the community on various issues, use COMMUNICATIONS  & COMMUNITY RELATIONS - COMMUNICATIONS.
examples_of_records: Statements; business cases; supporting information; working papers

category_description: POLICIES AND PROCEDURES - Development and establishment of decisions, directions and precedents, to support future decision making and operations.
examples_of_records: Final policies; final procedures; working papers where they are likely to be reused

category_description: REPORTING - Provision of formal responses to a requirement, situation or request (internal or external), e.g. reports to regulatory bodies, results of investigations, statistics. Includes public reporting such as the school's Annual Report.
examples_of_records: School Performance Information reports; parent, staff and student surveys

category_description: CHILD PROTECTION - Frameworks, programs, reviews relating to child protection.  For individual cases, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - CHILD PROTECTION.
CORPORATE IDENTITY - Development and protection of the name, coat of arms, crest, motto, logo or seal of the school.

hierarchy: GOVERNANCE > LEGAL SERVICES > ADVICE > MAJOR ISSUES
category_description: MAJOR ISSUES - Advice on issues of public interest or controversy, or which results in a change to school policy

hierarchy: GOVERNANCE > LEGAL SERVICES > ADVICE > OTHER ISSUES – Advice on issues that are not of public interest or controversy
category_description: OTHER ISSUES – Advice on issues that are not of public interest or controversy

hierarchy: GOVERNANCE > LEGAL SERVICES > LITIGATION > MAJOR MATTERS
category_description: MAJOR MATTERS - Matters where legal precedents are set, or are of public interest or controversy, or lead to a change in school policy -  Permanent

hierarchy: GOVERNANCE > LEGAL SERVICES > LITIGATION > OTHER MATTERS
category_description: OTHER MATTERS - that are not of public interest or controversy
FINANCIAL RECORDS - ANNUAL - Annual summaries of the school's financial position that are audited or used for other reporting
examples_of_records: Annual financial statements, statutory financial reports, balance sheets, operating statements, cash flow statements and accompanying notes

category_description: ACCOUNTING - Accounting records and associated supporting records related to managing the school's revenue and expenditure. Includes accounts payable and receivable, (including bequests, grant funding, donations, fundraising), banking records, payroll, debt recovery.  For records such as employee group certificates and tax declaration forms, use HUMAN RESOURCES - SALARIES.  For fund raising activities, use SCHOOL-RELATED GROUPS - ACTIVITIES
disposal_action: Destroy 7 years after end of financial year in which the record was created
examples_of_records: Accounts payable; accounts receivable; routine financial statements and reports; credit notices; petty cash records; cash books; journals; ledgers and other records of prime entry; bank deposit records; bank statements; bank reconciliation statements; investment and dividend statements; credit card statements; interim financial statements

category_description: BANKING - Establishment and closure of bank accounts, including investment accounts.  For records of banking transactions, use ADMINISTRATION - FINANCE - ACCOUNTING.
AUDIT - Conduct and reporting of financial or performance audits by internal and external parties. For the statutory School Performance Information report, use GOVERNANCE - REPORTING.
disposal_action: Destroy 7 years after audit results accepted
examples_of_records: Arrangements for audit; reports

category_description: REGISTER - Summary listing the school's assets.
examples_of_records: Asset register

category_description: VALUATIONS - asset valuation and asset management processes.
examples_of_records: Advices for inclusion on the asset register, valuation reports

category_description: BUDGETING - Compilation of annual budgets
disposal_action: Destroy 7 years after end of financial year in which the record was created
examples_of_records: Budget calculations; final budget

category_description: FUNDING - Successful and unsuccessful applications for grant or other funding. For the management of funding received, use ADMINISTRATION - FINANCE - ACCOUNTING.
disposal_action: Destroy 7 years after end of financial year to which the application relates
examples_of_records: Submissions; agreements; supporting correspondence

category_description: FEES - Setting of fees and charges for school services, programs, etc
disposal_action: Destroy 7 years after end of financial year to which the fees relate
examples_of_records: Calculations; final fee schedule; exceptions

category_description: TAXATION - Administration of taxation matters, including income tax, land tax, payroll tax, goods and services tax, fringe benefits tax and any other taxes. Includes payment of taxes.  For records such as employee group certificates and tax declaration forms, use ADMINISTRATION - HUMAN RESOURCES - SALARIES.
disposal_action: Destroy 5 years after end of financial year in which the record was created
examples_of_records: Tax assessments, instalment notices, payments.

category_description: POLICIES - Administration of various insurances taken out by the school, including property, contents, vehicles, public liability, professional indemnity, workers compensation.
CLAIMS - Management of claims against school insurance policies, other than Workers Compensation claims.  For  Workers Compensation claims, use ADMINISTRATION - OH&S - COMPENSATION.

ACQUISITION AND DISPOSAL - Acquisition and disposal of land or buildings. Includes acquisition through purchase, donation, bequest or other forms of acquisition. For acquisition and disposal of assets other than land or buildings, use ADMINISTRATION  PROPERTY- EQUIPMENT AND STORES.
examples_of_records: Legal documents relating to a purchase / sale; particulars of sale documents; board of survey; deeds and certificates; conditions of contracts; certificates of approval; photographs

category_description: CONSTRUCTION AND RENOVATION - Building a new structure, or undertaking renovation that affects the overall structure or design of the property, including grounds.  For routine refurbishment of property that does not affect its overall structure or design, use ADMINISTRATION - PROPERTY - REFURBISHMENT.  For contracts/agreements relating to the construction, major renovation or repair of buildings, grounds and facilities, use GOVERNANCE - AGREEMENTS.
examples_of_records: Tender documentation; heritage and environmental reports; drawings;, plans; specifications; submissions to planning authorities; soil tests; photographs

category_description: REFURBISHMENT - Routine refurbishment of property that does not affect its overall structure or design
MAINTENANCE - Upkeep, repair and maintenance of property, including e.g. cleaning, painting, repairs, grounds maintenance and electrical maintenance.
examples_of_records: Operating and maintenance manuals, test results and service log sheets for equipment including cooling towers; commissioning of refurbishment works

category_description: REGISTER - Summary of hazardous substances (including asbestos) identifying substance properties and their condition.

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > PROPERTY > HAZARDOUS MATERIALS > BUILDING MATERIALS
category_description: BUILDING MATERIALS - Identification, removal and disposal of hazardous materials from the fabric of a building, e.g. asbestos
disposal_action: Destroy 100 years after removal of hazardous materials

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > PROPERTY > HAZARDOUS MATERIALS > NON-BUILDING MATERIALS
category_description: NON-BUILDING MATERIALS - Removal, storage and disposal of hazardous waste and materials that are not from the fabric of building, e.g. chemicals or pesticides.
disposal_action: Destroy 30 years after removal of hazardous waste

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > PROPERTY > HAZARDOUS MATERIALS > NON-TOXIC WASTE
category_description: NON-TOXIC WASTE - Management and disposal of non-toxic waste.
disposal_action: Destroy 2 years after waste disposed of

category_description: SECURITY - Measures taken to protect people, premises, equipment or facilities from accidental or intentional damage or from unauthorised access.  For security of information systems, use ADMINISTRATION - INFORMATION MANAGEMENT-INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY AND SYSTEMS-MAINTENANCE.  For security checks on staff, volunteers or contractors, use ADMINISTRATION - HUMAN RESOURCES - STAFF HISTORY.
examples_of_records: Records of accidents and damage to property; records of incidents of illegal entry resulting in damage and/or theft; register of keys

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > PROPERTY > EQUIPMENT AND STORES > ACQUISITION
category_description: ACQUISITION - Acquisition of equipment and stores where there is no tender or contracting-out process. For those requiring tenders or contracts, use ADMINISTRATION -  AGREEMENTS.  For the financial transactions, use ADMINISTRATION -FINANCE- ACCOUNTING
examples_of_records: Requests for quotations; orders; handover reports; correspondence

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > PROPERTY > EQUIPMENT AND STORES > DISPOSAL
category_description: DISPOSAL - Disposal of equipment and stores no longer required, by sale, transfer, termination of lease, auction, donation or destruction.

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > PROPERTY > EQUIPMENT AND STORES > MAINTENANCE
category_description: MAINTENANCE - Upkeep, repair, servicing, modification and preservation of internal/external condition of equipment
examples_of_records: Service and maintenance records

category_description: FACILITY HIRE - Hiring out of school facilities (e.g. hall, sports centre) for non-school purposes.
disposal_action: Destroy 7 years after hiring arrangement expired

category_description: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY - Evidence of rights and entitlements over intellectual property owned by the school.

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > INFORMATION MANAGEMENT > INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY AND SYSTEMS > ACQUISITION
category_description: ACQUISITION - Acquisition of technology and telecommunication equipment, systems and services, where there is no tender or contracting-out process.  Includes licences. For services or systems contracted out, use ADMINISTRATION - AGREEMENTS.
disposal_action: Destroy 7 years after equipment, system or services are decommissioned.

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > INFORMATION MANAGEMENT > INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY AND SYSTEMS > IMPLEMENTATION
category_description: IMPLEMENTATION - Implementation of hardware, communications and systems. Includes preliminary studies, system development, testing, modification.
disposal_action: Destroy 5 years after implementation completed and accepted
examples_of_records: Feasibility studies; technical manuals; business rules; user requirements; system specifications

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > INFORMATION MANAGEMENT > INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY AND SYSTEMS > MANAGEMENT
category_description: MANAGEMENT - Ongoing management and maintenance of information technology and systems, including database management. Includes system controls, configuration management, security /protection from damage or unauthorised access, routine fixes and upgrades.
disposal_action: Destroy 7 years after equipment or system is decommissioned.

category_description: BREACHES - Management of breaches detected, or arising from a complaint.
examples_of_records: Complaints; investigations; reports

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > INFORMATION MANAGEMENT > PRIVACY > UNFOUNDED COMPLAINTS
category_description: UNFOUNDED COMPLAINTS -  Management of complaints that do not result in a privacy breach.
examples_of_records: Complaints; investigations; reports

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > INFORMATION MANAGEMENT > RECORDKEEPING AND ARCHIVES > CONTROLS
category_description: CONTROLS - Master control records for recordkeeping systems and archival collections.
examples_of_records: Information systems; registers; indexes; system configuration manuals; records destruction lists

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > INFORMATION MANAGEMENT > RECORDKEEPING AND ARCHIVES > REFERENCE SERVICES
category_description: REFERENCE SERVICES - Provision of research and reference services in response to internal and external enquiries or specific projects. For material that is used elsewhere, e.g. in a school publication, use EPHEMERA.
examples_of_records: Enquires and requests for information; research results; responses; summaries

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > INFORMATION MANAGEMENT > RECORDKEEPING AND ARCHIVES > DONATIONS AND LOANS
category_description: DONATIONS AND LOANS - Formal arrangements for receipt or refusal of donations or loans of material from external sources.
examples_of_records: Deeds of gift; loan agreements; collection management policy

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > INFORMATION MANAGEMENT > RECORDKEEPING AND ARCHIVES > CONSERVATION
category_description: CONSERVATION - Activities involved in the preservation, protection, maintenance, restoration and enhancement of information resources and artefacts.

STORAGE - Management of the storage of information resources including off-site or cloud storage. For the commissioning of storage services, use ADMINISTRATION - AGREEMENTS. For financial records, use ADMINISTRATION - FINANCE – ACCOUNTING
disposal_action: Destroy 7 years after administrative use has concluded.

category_description: AGREEMENTS - Establishment, maintenance, review and negotiation of enterprise workplace agreements set by the school.
AGREEMENTS - Establishment and maintenance of enterprise workplace agreements set by a peak body.
POSITIONS - Creation, variation, abolition of positions and assigned duties.
examples_of_records: Organisation charts; position descriptions (or equivalent)

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > HUMAN RESOURCES > RECRUITMENT > RECRUITMENT PROCESS
category_description: RECRUITMENT PROCESS -  for all positions, e.g. shortlisting, arranging interviews etc.
examples_of_records: Copies of applications; interview schedule; shortlists of applicants

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > HUMAN RESOURCES > RECRUITMENT > UNSUCCESSFUL APPLICATIONS.  For successful applications, use ADMINISTRATION
category_description: UNSUCCESSFUL APPLICATIONS.  For successful applications, use ADMINISTRATION - HUMAN RESOURCES - STAFF HISTORY
examples_of_records: Position description; application; evidence of registration and relevant checks; contract; employment history

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > HUMAN RESOURCES > STAFF HISTORY > SUMMARY
category_description: SUMMARY - Summary data for individual staff members, contractors and volunteers.  Summary includes name of individual, date of birth, date of appointment, date of separation, rates of salary and allowances, individual employment agreement, position titles, dates positions were held,  teacher and non-practising teacher registration, security check completion, evidence of professional learning, grievances relating to employment conditions, disciplinary matters.
examples_of_records: Human resources management system; Register of register of family day care educators, educator assistants and  coordinators as per the Education and Care Services National Law

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > HUMAN RESOURCES > STAFF HISTORY > EMPLOYMENT HISTORY
category_description: EMPLOYMENT HISTORY - SUMMARY MAINTAINED - Employment history records for individual staff members, contractors and volunteers where a summary record is also maintained.
disposal_action: Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Position description; application; evidence of registration and relevant checks; contract; employment history

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > HUMAN RESOURCES > STAFF HISTORY > EMPLOYMENT HISTORY
category_description: EMPLOYMENT HISTORY - SUMMARY NOT MAINTAINED - Employment history for individual staff members, contractors and volunteers where a summary record is not maintained.
examples_of_records: Position description; application; evidence of registration and relevant checks; contract; employment history

category_description: SECURITY CHECKS - Security checks (e.g. criminal history, Working with Children) carried out as part of pre-engagement, pre-employment and pre-appointment checks of staff, contractors, volunteers, employers of work experience students, religious instructors or any other person likely to be in contact with students. Includes periodic reviews.
disposal_action: Destroy 7 years after the person has left the school, providing the identifying number of the check, expiry date and date sighted by the school are retained in accordance with  ADMINISTRATION – HUMAN RESOURCES - STAFF HISTORY - SUMMARY
examples_of_records: Working With Children Checks; criminal history checks

category_description: COUNSELLING - Provision of advice or guidance to staff for various reasons, including trauma counselling.

SALARIES - Documentation of employees' salaries. Includes taxation declaration records, group certificates, payroll deduction authorities, records relating to the recovery of overpayments, and employee pay history and leave records.  For payroll records, use ADMINISTRATION - FINANCE - ACCOUNTING.
disposal_action: Destroy 75 years from date of birth

category_description: ALLOWANCES - Arranging and managing money paid to employees in addition to salary to cover expenses, e.g. travel allowances. For payroll records, use ADMINISTRATION - FINANCE - ACCOUNTING

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > HUMAN RESOURCES > STAFF DEVELOPMENT > CONFERENCES AND SEMINARS
category_description: CONFERENCES AND SEMINARS - Staff attendance at external conferences, seminars or similar
examples_of_records: Registration forms, conference materials and proceedings, participants' reports

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > HUMAN RESOURCES > STAFF DEVELOPMENT > EVALUATION and PLANNING
category_description: EVALUATION and PLANNING - Assessment of staff development needs, services or programs available, development and implementation of staff development plans.
examples_of_records: Training needs analysis; staff development plans

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > HUMAN RESOURCES > STAFF DEVELOPMENT > MASTER RECORD of accredited or approved courses, programs, training packages, modules and units provided for staff of the school, or of other schools.  For the approval of courses, use GOVERNANCE
category_description: MASTER RECORD of accredited or approved courses, programs, training packages, modules and units provided for staff of the school, or of other schools.  For the approval of courses, use GOVERNANCE - REGULATION.
disposal_action: Destroy 7 years after accreditation expires or is revoked, or course is discontinued
examples_of_records: Summary of curriculum content; syllabus; teaching objectives; assessment methods;  course, subject or unit descriptions and delivery methods

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > OCCUPATIONAL HEALTH AND SAFETY (OH&S) > INCIDENTS
category_description: INCIDENTS - Investigation and reporting on dangerous occurrences, any accidents or incidents where personal injury and incapacity of a staff member,  contractor or visitor occurred on the school's premises, or for staff and contractors while travelling on duty.  For all claims for workers compensation, use ADMINISTRATION - OH&S - COMPENSATION.
disposal_action: Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Accident register; reports

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > OCCUPATIONAL HEALTH AND SAFETY (OH&S) > COMPENSATION
category_description: COMPENSATION - Management of claims for compensation submitted for personal injury of a staff member, contractor, volunteer or visitor. Includes the rehabilitation of injured workers.
disposal_action: Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Incident reports; results of investigations; claims; medical details; correspondence with insurer; return to work plans

hierarchy: ADMINISTRATION > OCCUPATIONAL HEALTH AND SAFETY (OH&S) > AUDIT
category_description: AUDIT - Conduct of external or internal OH&S audits and inspections
ROSTERING - Allocation of teachers or supervisors to camps, visits, sporting events, excursions etc
examples_of_records: Rosters

category_description: ENQUIRIES - Enquiries and messages of appreciation or suggestions from external sources (e.g. parents of prospective students, the public).
COMPLIANCE - Meeting of regulatory requirements, including registration of food premises, assessments, inspections, food safety program, incident (e.g. contamination, poisoning) management.
examples_of_records: Registration certificates (or equivalent); assessment reports; food safety program

category_description: OPERATIONS - Routine running of food services, including stock management, equipment monitoring
examples_of_records: Inventories; equipment monitoring results

hierarchy: STUDENT SERVICES > OUT OF HOURS CARE > ATTENDANCE
category_description: ATTENDANCE - Registration of students attending out of hours care.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth

hierarchy: STUDENT SERVICES > OUT OF HOURS CARE > ACTIVITIES
category_description: ACTIVITIES - Development and provision of programs, activities

hierarchy: STUDENT SERVICES > OUT OF HOURS CARE > EXTERNAL SERVICES
category_description: EXTERNAL SERVICES - Management and monitoring of external organisations providing of out of hours care  on behalf of the school. For all matters (e.g. incidents, health, activities) relating to the student use the relevant topic.  For the commissioning of services and final agreements/ contracts, use GOVERNANCE- AGREEMENTS.
examples_of_records: Tenders, appointment, contracts, service level agreements

hierarchy: STUDENT SERVICES > HEALTH AND WELFARE > PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT AND MONITORING
category_description: PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT AND MONITORING - Research, development and articulation of health and welfare programs for all students, or specific categories of student.
examples_of_records: Final versions of program, statistics and supporting data, monitoring and evaluation records, implementation plans

hierarchy: STUDENT SERVICES > HEALTH AND WELFARE > PROGRAM OPERATION
category_description: PROGRAM OPERATION - Routine delivery of a health and welfare program or project
disposal_action: Destroy 7 years after program ceases or is superseded
examples_of_records: Delivery plans, progress reports, meeting records

hierarchy: STUDENT SERVICES > HEALTH AND WELFARE > SUMMARY HEALTH DATA
category_description: SUMMARY HEALTH DATA - summary data relating to programs administered, e.g. vaccination.
examples_of_records: Vaccination register

category_description: SUCCESSFUL - Successful applications for accommodation services
examples_of_records: Applications, code of conduct

category_description: UNSUCCESSFUL - Withdrawn or unsuccessful applications for accommodation services.
examples_of_records: Applications

category_description: CAREERS - Provision of career advice, programs, information, services and events.  For work experience use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - WORK EXPERIENCE
LIBRARY - Provision of library services, including collection acquisition, management (including loans), de-accessioning and student and staff support.
examples_of_records: Collection management records, guides/ instructions, lists of acquisitions and disposals, loan records

hierarchy: STUDENT SERVICES > SCHOOL ANIMAL & CROP MANAGEMENT
category_description: SCHOOL ANIMAL & CROP MANAGEMENT - Activities relating to the breeding, care, use, protection and disposal of animals and animal body parts or specimens, and the planting, growth, maintenance, use, protection and disposal of plant material or specimens for educational purposes. For the management of hazardous materials, use ADMINISTRATION - PROPERTY - HAZARDOUS MATERIALS.
examples_of_records: Records relating to animal or property acquisition and disposal, animal identification, permits, agistment, veterinary and care, chemical management, disease and pest control, housing or storage facilities, transportation, quarantine, scientific use approval, plant disposal, soil tests, usage registers

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > STUDENT MASTER RECORD > Indigenous STUDENTS
category_description: Indigenous STUDENTS - Summary record for students of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander origin

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > STUDENT MASTER RECORD > STUDENTS IN OUT-OF-HOME-CARE
category_description: STUDENTS IN OUT-OF-HOME-CARE - Summary for students who are living, or have lived in orphanages, children's homes or with alternative care-givers.

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > STUDENT MASTER RECORD > OTHER STUDENTS
category_description: OTHER STUDENTS - Summary for students who are not Indigenous, or are living or have lived in out-of-home-care.
disposal_action: Destroy 75 years from date of birth

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > STUDENT MASTER RECORD > SUPPORTING RECORDS for STUDENT MASTER RECORD
category_description: SUPPORTING RECORDS for STUDENT MASTER RECORD - Supporting records for individual students covering a range of student management activities - where a summary exists
disposal_action: Destroy 25 years from date of birth

category_description: SUMMARY - Summary of admissions/enrolments, and summary of departures pre Year 12
examples_of_records: Admissions register (or equivalent)

category_description: INDIVIDUAL - SUCCESSFUL - Individual student enrolment records - successful
disposal_action: Destroy 7years after student leaves the school, and if a summary record exists
examples_of_records: Application and supporting documentation; decision; appeals

category_description: INDIVIDUAL - DEPARTURES  - Individual student departure records, pre Year 12
disposal_action: Destroy 25 years from date of birth, and if a summary record exists

category_description: INDIVIDUAL - UNSUCCESSFUL - Individual student enrolment records - unsuccessful - Applications for enrolment that are unsuccessful or the applicants did not commence at the school.
examples_of_records: Application and supporting documentation; decision; appeals; notifications and consents from parents;

category_description: ATTENDANCE - Management of student attendance and absences. Includes movement of students in and out of their accommodation. For behavioural issues relating to attendance, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - BEHAVIOUR.  If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Roll books/registers; attendance database; certificates of attendance; notices of non attendance; sign-in/ out register

category_description: ESTABLISHMENT - Establishment of financial assistance programs, e.g. a scholarship.

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE > PROGRAM MANAGEMENT
category_description: PROGRAM MANAGEMENT - Management and administration of financial assistance programs
disposal_action: Destroy 7 years after action completed and audit

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE > INDIVIDUAL AWARDS
category_description: INDIVIDUAL AWARDS - Award of financial assistance to specific students, e.g  a scholarship, an allowance.  For summary information use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.

ESTABLISHMENT - Establishment of awards. Includes communication with donors/sponsors, summary records of awards granted or posts held, successful applications, nominations, records of the decision making process.
SUMMARIES - of awards received by all students
examples_of_records: Annual lists, honour boards, registers of school captains and prefects

category_description: INDIVIDUAL AWARDS - Individual student awards, posts etc.  If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > HEALTH AND WELFARE > ACCIDENTS AND INCIDENTS > REGISTER
category_description: REGISTER - of accidents, injuries, incidents, illness and notifiable disease
examples_of_records: Accident register, first aid register, sick bay register, infectious disease register

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > HEALTH AND WELFARE > ACCIDENTS AND INCIDENTS > INVESTIGATION
category_description: INVESTIGATION - Reports and investigations of accidents, injuries, incidents and illness. Includes suspected event sustained at home in which the school becomes involved.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Investigations and reports

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > HEALTH AND WELFARE > STUDENT MEDICAL AND WELFARE HISTORY
category_description: STUDENT MEDICAL AND WELFARE HISTORY - Records of all physical, psychological treatment, pastoral care and support given to a student.  Includes counselling, advice and therapy.  If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Case notes, reports, assessments and observations, meeting notes, treatment records,

category_description: CASES - Notifications, allegations, investigations and reports, and any related records where a person reasonably suspects harm or a risk of harm to a student, including action taken.  Includes management of students subject to a relevant court order (e.g. child protection order)
examples_of_records: Reports - formal and informal, records of investigations, recommendations, documentation of action taken.  Supporting records, e.g. attendance, leave and travel records; personnel files; counselling or discipline records; referrals to and eviews of actions, cases or decisions by external authorities; records of claims, assessments, reviews and appeals for individuals; interventions, support or compensation and attempted or successful redress; and counselling, mediation and medical records

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > CHILD PROTECTION > HOST FAMILIES
category_description: HOST FAMILIES - Selection and screening of host families for exchange students and international  students. If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth

category_description: SIGNIFICANT EVENTS - Significant events, cases or activities, which may result in student suspensions, expulsions or prosecutions through Court.  If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth

category_description: ROUTINE EVENTS - Routine events, cases or activities
disposal_action: Destroy 6 years after student leaves the school

category_description: HOME SCHOOLING - Management of students seeking exemption to be schooled from home.  For records relating to a student while they are at school, use other classes under STUDENT MANAGEMENT. If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Copies of parent application to the relevant authority; student details; communications regarding approval/ non approval.

category_description: WORK EXPERIENCE - Arrangements for, and monitoring of individual student work experience or practical placements.  Includes screening of host employer and supervisors. If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Agreements with placement hosts, course/work structure, parent/guardian permissions, applications, evaluations, host reports

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > CO-CURRICULAR ACTIVITIES > PROGRAM ESTABLISHMENT
category_description: PROGRAM ESTABLISHMENT - Establishment and major review of specific programs. For minor reviews or changes, use the relevant CO-CURRICULAR ACTIVITY topic - ARRANGEMENTS
disposal_action: Destroy 2 years after program superseded or discontinued

category_description: HOUSE - Identification, membership, reports of houses. For activities use other topics under STUDENT MANAGEMENT - ACTIVITIES, or STUDENT MANAGEMENT - HEALTH AND WELFARE

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > CO-CURRICULAR ACTIVITIES > EXCURSIONS > ARRANGEMENTS
category_description: ARRANGEMENTS - Logistical and administrative arrangements for an excursion, visit etc.  For incidents affecting one or more students, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - HEALTH AND WELFARE
examples_of_records: Plans, schedules, transport bookings, advices to parents/ guardians

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > CO-CURRICULAR ACTIVITIES > EXCURSIONS > STUDENT PARTICIPATION
category_description: STUDENT PARTICIPATION - Records of participation of students in camps, visits etc.  If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Parent/guardian permissions, applications (e.g. for overseas trips), photographs

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > CO-CURRICULAR ACTIVITIES > SPORTS > ARRANGEMENTS
category_description: ARRANGEMENTS - Logistical and administrative arrangements.  For incidents affecting one or more students, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - HEALTH AND WELFARE
examples_of_records: Team lists, rosters, results

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > CO-CURRICULAR ACTIVITIES > SPORTS > STUDENT PARTICIPATION
category_description: STUDENT PARTICIPATION - Records of participation of students in sports and sporting events. If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Photographs, parent/ guardian permissions and approvals

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > CO-CURRICULAR ACTIVITIES > ARTS > ARRANGEMENTS
category_description: ARRANGEMENTS - Logistical and administrative arrangements.
examples_of_records: Venue and support arrangements, schedules, tickets, programs, photographs

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > CO-CURRICULAR ACTIVITIES > ARTS > STUDENT PARTICIPATION
category_description: STUDENT PARTICIPATION - Records of participation of students in artistic events. If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Photographs

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > CO-CURRICULAR ACTIVITIES > SERVICE LEARNING > ARRANGEMENTS
category_description: ARRANGEMENTS - Logistical and administrative arrangements.
examples_of_records: Activity plans, financial plans

hierarchy: STUDENT MANAGEMENT > CO-CURRICULAR ACTIVITIES > SERVICE LEARNING > STUDENT PARTICIPATION
category_description: STUDENT PARTICIPATION - Records of participation of students in community events. If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Parent/guardian permissions, photographs

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > CURRICULUM > MAJOR PROGRAMS
category_description: MAJOR PROGRAMS - Research and development of programs that have a substantial impact on the curriculum and its delivery
examples_of_records: Plans, programs, reports, final versions of the curriculum

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > CURRICULUM > DEVELOPMENT AND DELIVERY
category_description: DEVELOPMENT AND DELIVERY - Routine development and delivery of curriculum and teaching materials.  For trainees or apprentices that undertake a qualification with an external supervisor, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - MASTER STUDENT RECORD.
examples_of_records: Curriculum content, revisions to units, syllabus, course notes, working papers, teaching methods, subject lists, handbooks, class programs, lesson guides

category_description: REVIEW - regular or specific review or evaluation of curriculum and its delivery
examples_of_records: teacher observations

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > CURRICULUM > INTERNATIONAL BACCALAUREATE (IB)
category_description: INTERNATIONAL BACCALAUREATE (IB) - Management and delivery of the IB program and curriculum.  For student results, use TEACHING AND LEARNING - EXAMINATIONS, or ASSESSMENTS.  For becoming an authorised IB school, use GOVERNANCE - REGULATION.
examples_of_records: Curriculum, program structure, teaching methods, subject lists, handbooks, class program, lesson guides

category_description: DISTANCE EDUCATION - Program-level establishment and maintenance, including facilities, parent support.  For curriculum, use TEACHING AND LEARNING - CURRICULUM.  For assessment, use TEACHING AND LEARNING - ASSESSMENTS.  For student-related information use the relevant topic.
disposal_action: Destroy 2 years after the program is superseded
examples_of_records: Program structure, materials for parents, procedures

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > SPECIAL EDUCATION > PROGRAMS
category_description: PROGRAMS - Program-level design and management
examples_of_records: Program design, plans, reports, lists of registered students, engagement of support services

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > SPECIAL EDUCATION > INDIVIDUAL STUDENTS
category_description: INDIVIDUAL STUDENTS - Management of individual students
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Student work plans, referrals, teacher observations

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > HIGH POTENTIAL PROGRAMS > PROGRAMS
category_description: PROGRAMS - Program-level design and management
examples_of_records: Program design, plans, reports, lists of registered students, engagement of support services

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > HIGH POTENTIAL PROGRAMS > INDIVIDUAL STUDENTS
category_description: INDIVIDUAL STUDENTS - Management of individual students.  If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Student work plans, referrals, teacher observations

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > LEARNING ANALYTICS > AGGREGATED DATA AND ANALYSIS
category_description: AGGREGATED DATA AND ANALYSIS -  Data collected and analysed that is used for routine reporting, predictive modelling, identifying gaps and opportunities for improvement. For reports or other outcomes that affect curriculum and program development, use TEACHING AND LEARNING - CURRICULUM, OR TEACHING AND LEARNING - SPECIAL EDUCATION.
disposal_action: Destroy 3 years after obsolete or superseded

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > LEARNING ANALYTICS > INDIVIDUAL STUDENT
category_description: INDIVIDUAL STUDENT - Data and analysis for individual students. If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth

category_description: TIMETABLES - Establishment of class timetables. For rostering of staff on camps, visits, sporting events etc, use ADMINISTRATION - HUMAN RESOURCES - ROSTERING

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > EXAMINATIONS & ASSESSMENTS > SUMMARY RESULTS
category_description: SUMMARY RESULTS - QUALIFICATIONS -  Summary of results for years 11 and 12, IB students, apprentices and trainees for each year.

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > EXAMINATIONS & ASSESSMENTS > SUMMARY RESULTS
category_description: SUMMARY RESULTS - OTHER YEARS  - Summary of results or examinations and assessments for years other than 11 and 12.

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > EXAMINATIONS & ASSESSMENTS > INDIVIDUAL STUDENTS
category_description: INDIVIDUAL STUDENTS - RESULTS - Record of individual student assessment and achievement, including student reports. Includes completion or partial completion of traineeships and apprenticeships. If a summary record is maintained, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - STUDENT MASTER RECORD.  If there is no summary record use this class.
disposal_action: Students of Indigenous origin or in out-of-home care - Retain permanently.  Other students - Destroy 75 years from date of birth
examples_of_records: Reports, results, statements of attainment, training contracts/agreements

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > EXAMINATIONS & ASSESSMENTS > CERTIFICATES
category_description: CERTIFICATES - Copies of certificates, academic transcripts given to students, parents or guardians. For the Year 12 School Reference, use STUDENT MANAGEMENT - MASTER STUDENT RECORD.
examples_of_records: Copies of certificates, academic transcripts or similar, requests for transcripts

hierarchy: TEACHING AND LEARNING > EXAMINATIONS & ASSESSMENTS > ARRANGEMENTS
category_description: ARRANGEMENTS - Administrative arrangements for setting, holding and supervision of examinations or other assessments, including examination/ assessment materials.
examples_of_records: Timetables, exam sheets, test papers,  assignments, answer books, marking guides, supervision rosters, special consideration requests

hierarchy: COMMUNICATIONS  & COMMUNITY RELATIONS > CIRCULARS AND INSTRUCTIONS > ROUTINE INTERNAL
category_description: ROUTINE INTERNAL - Routine internal staff notices and communications. For notices about significant events, use the relevant topic.
disposal_action: Destroy 2 years after action completed or instruction superseded

hierarchy: COMMUNICATIONS  & COMMUNITY RELATIONS > CIRCULARS AND INSTRUCTIONS > EXTERNAL
category_description: EXTERNAL - Circulars from the school including to parents/ guardians.
PRODUCTION - Development, design and production of school publications.
examples_of_records: Designs, content, drafts, working papers, publication arrangements

category_description: PUBLICATION - Final copy of a school publication. For formal group photographs, use OBJECTS.
examples_of_records: School magazine, year books, school histories, photographs, printed publications, web publications, audiotapes, films, videos, fliers, posters.

category_description: ARRANGEMENTS - Planning, arranging and facilitating events.
examples_of_records: Invitations, notifications of events, acceptances

hierarchy: COMMUNICATIONS  & COMMUNITY RELATIONS > CELEBRATIONS > RECORDS OF EVENT
category_description: RECORDS OF EVENT - Records of the event, produced for the event or as an outcome of the event.
examples_of_records: Photographs, recordings, speeches, programs, posters, advertising

hierarchy: COMMUNICATIONS  & COMMUNITY RELATIONS > VISITS TO THE SCHOOL > VIPs
category_description: VIPs - Visits by eminent persons, e.g. royalty, minister of state, famous sports, entertainment or cultural people.
examples_of_records: Visitor books, photographs, speeches

hierarchy: COMMUNICATIONS  & COMMUNITY RELATIONS > VISITS TO THE SCHOOL > ARRANGEMENTS
category_description: ARRANGEMENTS  - Administrative arrangements for visits by VIPs and all other persons or organisations.
examples_of_records: Visitor books, photographs, records of administrative and logistical arrangements

category_description: COMMUNITY RELATIONS - Communication between the school and the wider community, including the media.  Includes marketing campaigns, open days, handling of public reaction to the school's policy or actions, addresses at public events, social media activities.   For grievances from parents/guardians, use the relevant topic.
examples_of_records: Complaints, congratulations, speeches, programs, display materials, advertising.

category_description: GOVERNANCE - The Group's establishment and governance
examples_of_records: Constitution, rules/polices/procedures, minutes of meetings, reports to the school Council (or equivalent).

category_description: MEMBERSHIP - Registers of Group members
examples_of_records: Membership lists

category_description: ACTIVITIES - Management of the Group's activities, e.g. planning and organising fund raising, reunions, commemorations. For financial records of funds received from external sources, use ADMINISTRATION - FINANCE - ACCOUNTING.

hierarchy: SPIRITUAL PROGRAMS > RELIGIOUS CEREMONIES AND SERVICES > Summary records of baptisms, confirmations, marriages,  funerals and memorials.
category_description: Summary records of baptisms, confirmations, marriages,  funerals and memorials.
examples_of_records: Registers, indexes, lists of participants

hierarchy: SPIRITUAL PROGRAMS > RELIGIOUS CEREMONIES AND SERVICES > ARRANGEMENTS
category_description: ARRANGEMENTS  - Administrative arrangements for ceremonies and services.
